real_insight: When you find yourself blocked on touching a system out of respect for the person who built it, ask: would I be blocked if a stranger had built it? If yes, the loyalty is the right kind. If no, the loyalty is the wrong kind.
objection: I have personally watched this exact failure mode play out — the senior backend engineer who keeps refusing to deprecate the principal's auth service while couching every objection in respectful language ('foundational code,' 'subtleties only they would understand'). The verse names the trap with precision and supplies a one-line test that actually distinguishes refusal-as-inquiry from refusal-as-deferral. The 'three CVEs that cannot be patched' detail and the 'each objection sincere; none survives technical review' detail give the verse the texture of a real engineering observation rather than aphorism. PASS. The implication line is genuinely useful — I will steal it.
